heroHeadline: Balikatan 2026 Day 6: China Flanks Luzon From East as Task Group 107 Conducts Live-Fire Drills; COC Unlikely at May 5–9 ASEAN Summit
heroSubtitle: China's PLA Southern Theater Command escalated its Balikatan 2026 counter-posture on April 24–25 by deploying Naval Task Group 107 to conduct live-fire shooting, sea-air coordination, rapid maneuvers, and maritime replenishment exercises in waters east of Luzon Island — directly flanking the Philippines from the Philippine Sea side while 17,500+ allied troops conduct Balikatan 41-2026 drills through May 8. The PLA characterized the operation as 'a necessary action in response to the current regional situation.' This eastern-flank deployment completes China's four-axis counter-demonstration against Balikatan: the Liaoning carrier group Taiwan Strait transit (April 20), the 133rd Task Group Western Pacific circuit (April 22), the Type 076 Sichuan drone carrier SCS sea trials (ongoing), and now Task Group 107 east of Luzon — encircling the Philippine archipelago with simultaneous PLA naval activities. On the diplomatic front, analysts warned April 24 that the 48th ASEAN Summit (May 5–9, Cebu) is unlikely to finalize a binding South China Sea Code of Conduct despite Philippine DFA Secretary Lazaro calling it 'something we owe the world.' Singapore's RSIS assessed only 'incremental progress' at the summit, while Stanford's Ray Powell noted China lacks the will to accept any authority above its own political judgment. Balikatan 2026 remains on track through May 8 with all seven nations active; the upcoming SINKEX against BRP Quezon will be the exercise's most visible trilateral demonstration of US-Japan-Philippines sea-denial capability.
footerNote: Data sourced from CSIS AMTI, Philippine DFA/AFP/PCG, US Dept. of Defense/DVIDS, USNI News, Reuters, AP, South China Morning Post, Al Jazeera, Rappler, GMA News, Manila Bulletin, Manila Times, Tribune Philippines, Philstar, Stars and Stripes, The Diplomat, US News, Free Malaysia Today, Radio Free Asia
